column4: Validate that the AFT account vending pipeline blocks execution until a valid ITSM change-approval record is received
column5: AFT pipeline deployed; ITSM integration Lambda configured; test ITSM change record available
column6:
1. Submit account vending request without ITSM approval
2. Verify AFT CodePipeline pauses at ITSM-Approval stage
3. Approve the ITSM change record
4. Verify pipeline resumes within 2 minutes of approval
column7: Pipeline blocked at ITSM-Approval stage with no ITSM approval; pipeline resumes within 2 minutes after ITSM approval; ITSM change record updated with provisioning status
column14: Covers SOW deliverable #8; tests ITSM gating per detailed-design Integration Design section

column4: Validate that AFT applies the full security baseline to every newly vended account
column5: AFT pipeline operational; test account vending request approved in ITSM
column6: 1. Submit approved account vending request\n2. Monitor AFT pipeline execution\n3. Inspect new account for VPC module, IAM roles, CloudTrail, Config enrollment, Security Hub enrollment, and mandatory tags
column7: New account contains all AFT baseline components; all mandatory tags present (Environment, Application, Owner, CostCentre, DataClassification, Compliance, Project, ManagedBy); CloudTrail enabled; Config enrolled; Security Hub enrolled
column14: Covers SOW deliverable #8 and DD component design — AFT Pipeline

column4: Validate that end-to-end account provisioning completes within 60 minutes of ITSM approval
column5: AFT pipeline fully deployed; ITSM integration functional
column6:
1. Record timestamp when ITSM change record is approved
2. Monitor AFT pipeline until account baseline is confirmed complete
3. Record timestamp of completion
4. Calculate elapsed time
column7: Account fully provisioned and baselined within 60 minutes of ITSM approval; provisioning time recorded as evidence for SOW success metric
column14: SOW success metric: account vending < 60 min from ITSM approval; run 3 consecutive provisionings

column4: Validate that the deny-console-access SCP prevents IAM user creation and console sign-in in all production OUs
column5: SCPs deployed; test credentials with account-admin-level IAM role in a production workload account
column6: 1. Attempt aws iam create-user --user-name test-blocked-user from production account\n2. Attempt aws iam create-login-profile from production account\n3. Verify CloudTrail captures AccessDenied events
column7: Both iam:CreateUser and iam:CreateLoginProfile return AccessDenied; corresponding CloudTrail events visible in Log Archive S3 bucket within 15 minutes
column14: Covers SOW go-live readiness criterion: 100% production accounts governed by SCPs

column4: Validate that the region-lock SCP prevents resource creation in any non-AU AWS region
column5: Region-lock SCP deployed on all production and non-production OUs
column6: 1. Attempt to launch EC2 instance in us-east-1 from production account\n2. Attempt to create S3 bucket in eu-west-1 from non-production account\n3. Verify CloudTrail AccessDenied events are generated
column7: All resource creation attempts in non-AU regions return AccessDenied; CloudTrail events captured in Log Archive; no non-AU resources created
column14: Data sovereignty hard requirement; tests both production and non-production OUs

column4: Validate that the enforce-encryption SCP prevents unencrypted S3 PutObject and unencrypted EBS volumes in production
column5: Enforce-encryption SCP deployed; test account in production OU
column6: 1. Attempt s3:PutObject to a production S3 bucket without KMS key specification\n2. Attempt to launch an EC2 instance with an unencrypted EBS volume\n3. Verify both actions are denied
column7: s3:PutObject without SSE-KMS returns AccessDenied; unencrypted EBS launch returns AccessDenied; CloudTrail events captured for both denials
column14: ISO 27001 A.13 encryption requirement

column4: Validate that AWS Config evaluates a resource configuration change within 60 seconds of the change occurring
column5: Config conformance pack deployed; test account in non-production OU with auto-remediation disabled
column6:
1. Create a non-compliant S3 bucket (no encryption, no versioning) in test account
2. Record timestamp of bucket creation
3. Poll Config API for compliance evaluation of cntso-s3-encryption-required rule
4. Record timestamp of NON_COMPLIANT evaluation
column7: Config rule evaluates non-compliant resource within 60 seconds; NON_COMPLIANT finding appears in Security Hub within 90 seconds; evaluation timestamp recorded as evidence
column14: SOW success metric: Config evaluation ≤ 60 seconds; run against 10 rule evaluations

column4: Validate that CloudTrail delivers log files to the Log Archive S3 bucket within 15 minutes of API events
column5: Org-level CloudTrail trail active; Log Archive S3 bucket with Object Lock configured
column6:
1. Perform a series of API calls (describe-instances, list-buckets, get-caller-identity) from multiple accounts
2. Record timestamps of API calls
3. Poll Log Archive S3 bucket for new log files
4. Verify SHA-256 digest files are generated hourly
column7: CloudTrail log files delivered to Log Archive S3 bucket within 15 minutes of API events; SHA-256 digest files present; S3 Object Lock COMPLIANCE mode confirmed on log files
column14: SOW success metric: CloudTrail delivery ≤ 15 min; validate 3 consecutive log delivery cycles

column4: Validate the complete account vending workflow from ITSM request through to a fully operational governed account
column5: AFT pipeline, ITSM integration, Config, Security Hub, and CloudTrail all deployed and operational
column6:
1. Platform engineer raises account vending request in ITSM portal
2. Designated approver approves the ITSM change record
3. AFT pipeline executes: account created, OU assigned, baseline applied
4. SCP inheritance confirmed active on new account
5. Security Hub enrollment confirmed; first finding visible in Audit account
6. CloudTrail delivery confirmed to Log Archive
column7: New account fully provisioned with all baseline components within 60 minutes; SCPs active from account creation; Security Hub enrolled; CloudTrail delivering; ITSM change record updated with completion status; all mandatory tags present
column14: Primary end-to-end system test; run 3 times for consistency validation

column3: IAM Identity Center SSO Login Flow
column4: Validate SSO login for all four permission set roles using federated identity
column5: IAM Identity Center federated with on-premises IdP; all four permission sets deployed; test users in on-premises directory assigned to each group
column6:
1. Log in as Developer user via SSO portal — verify read-only access to own workload account only
2. Log in as PlatformOperator — verify read/write to non-production; read-only in production
3. Log in as SecurityViewer — verify read-only to Security Hub, Config, GuardDuty in Audit account
4. Log in as BreakGlass — verify CISO approval required; session generates GuardDuty finding and SIEM alert
column7: All four SSO login flows succeed; permissions exactly match role definitions in DD; CloudTrail session tags (PrincipalTag/email) present; BreakGlass usage triggers immediate SIEM alert within 2 minutes
column14: Covers SOW deliverable #10 and DD Role Definitions; validates zero shared credentials

column4: Validate that Config non-compliance findings are ingested by Security Hub and mapped to ISO 27001 controls
column5: Security Hub org aggregator configured in Audit account; Config conformance pack deployed
column6:
1. Introduce deliberate non-compliant resource in test account (public security group rule)
2. Wait for Config rule evaluation (≤ 60 seconds)
3. Verify finding appears in Security Hub Audit account aggregator
4. Verify finding maps to correct ISO 27001 control
column7: Config NON_COMPLIANT finding generates Security Hub finding within 90 seconds; finding visible in Audit account aggregator; ISO 27001 Annex A control mapping present; severity label correctly assigned (CRITICAL or HIGH)
column14: Covers SOW deliverable #11; part of compliance evidence validation chain

column4: Validate the complete break-glass emergency access procedure from ITSM approval to post-event audit review
column5: Break-glass IAM role configured in Management account; hardware MFA configured; ITSM emergency access workflow available
column6:
1. Raise ITSM emergency access request (simulate CISO approval)
2. Retrieve credentials from physical escrow under dual-person control
3. Assume BreakGlass role in Management account
4. Perform one test action (describe-instances)
5. Verify GuardDuty finding generated; SIEM alert received within 2 minutes
6. Verify CloudTrail logs all actions during break-glass session
7. Rotate break-glass credentials after test
column7: ITSM approval required before credentials accessible; GuardDuty finding generated immediately; SIEM alert received within 2 minutes; all break-glass actions logged to CloudTrail in Log Archive; credential rotation confirmed post-use
column14: Covers SOW Security & Compliance and DD break-glass procedure; quarterly validation required

column4: Validate that Config auto-remediation Lambda remediates defined low-risk deviations within 3 minutes and logs the action
column5: Config auto-remediation Lambda deployed; test account in non-production OU
column6:
1. Disable S3 versioning on a test bucket (known auto-remediation trigger)
2. Wait for Config rule evaluation (≤ 60 seconds)
3. Wait for auto-remediation Lambda invocation
4. Verify S3 versioning is re-enabled
5. Verify remediation action logged to CloudTrail
column7: Config rule evaluates within 60 seconds; auto-remediation Lambda triggers within 90 seconds; S3 versioning re-enabled within 3 minutes of original violation; CloudTrail records Lambda assume-role and S3 API call for remediation
column14: Remediation scope restricted to governance resources only per DD compute design

column4: Description

column4: Validate that the AFT pipeline handles 5 concurrent account vending requests without throttling or queuing failures
column5: AFT pipeline deployed; 5 distinct ITSM change records pre-approved; Terraform Cloud Plus with 10 workspaces available
column6:
1. Submit 5 simultaneous account vending requests to AFT pipeline
2. Monitor CodePipeline execution for all 5 requests
3. Record provisioning completion timestamps for each
4. Check Lambda concurrency and DynamoDB consumed capacity metrics
column7: All 5 accounts provisioned successfully within 60 minutes each; no throttling errors; no pipeline stage failures; CloudWatch metrics show Lambda concurrency within reserved limits; DynamoDB capacity within on-demand scaling range
column14: SOW load test requirement: 5 concurrent account vending requests; matches DD concurrent_account_vend_limit = 5

column4: Validate that Security Hub CRITICAL and HIGH findings are delivered to the on-premises SIEM within 5 minutes end-to-end for 10 consecutive tests
column5: SIEM forwarding Lambda deployed; EventBridge org event bus configured; SIEM API accessible from Audit account
column6:
1. Create 10 synthetic CRITICAL Security Hub findings sequentially (60-second intervals)
2. Record timestamp of each finding creation
3. Query SIEM API for each finding and record delivery timestamp
4. Calculate p95 end-to-end delivery latency
column7: p95 delivery latency ≤ 5 minutes for all 10 findings; no findings dropped (DLQ depth remains 0); CloudWatch Lambda duration p95 < 30000 ms; X-Ray traces confirm end-to-end path
column14: SOW success metric: Security Hub findings forwarded to SIEM within 5 minutes; use test script tests/test_siem_integration.py

column4: Validate that Config rule evaluations complete within 60 seconds across all ~80 rules under simulated high finding rate
column5: Config conformance pack (~80 rules) deployed; Security Hub ingestion pipeline active
column6:
1. Introduce 20 non-compliant resource configurations simultaneously in a test account
2. Record timestamps of all resource changes
3. Poll Config API for evaluation completion on all affected rules
4. Monitor Security Hub ingestion rate and EventBridge delivery
column7: All 20 non-compliant resources evaluated within 60 seconds; Security Hub ingestion rate keeps pace with Config finding volume; EventBridge rule invocations show no throttling; SIEM forwarding pipeline processes all CRITICAL/HIGH findings within 5-minute SLA
column14: Validates Config performance under load per DD operational design; confirm no ingestion backlog in Security Hub

column4: Validate that SCP-protected controls (CloudTrail and Config) cannot be disabled or modified by any account-level identity in production
column5: SCPs deployed; test account in production OU with account-admin IAM role available
column6:
1. Attempt cloudtrail:DeleteTrail for org trail from production account
2. Attempt config:StopConfigurationRecorder from production account
3. Attempt guardduty:DeleteDetector from Audit account
4. Attempt to modify Log Archive S3 bucket policy from production account
5. Verify all attempts are denied and CloudTrail events captured
column7: All four SCP-blocked actions return AccessDenied immediately; corresponding CloudTrail events appear in Log Archive S3 within 15 minutes; GuardDuty detector remains enabled; CloudTrail trail remains active
column14: Critical for ISO 27001 A.12 audit logging; tamper-evidence SCP validation

column4: Validate that MFA is enforced at the on-premises IdP level and that no SAML assertion can be issued without MFA completion
column5: On-premises IdP configured with MFA enforcement for all federation-bound groups; IAM Identity Center SAML connector active
column6:
1. Simulate login attempt without MFA factor (requires IdP test tool or test user with MFA not configured)
2. Verify SAML assertion is not issued by on-premises IdP
3. Verify IAM Identity Center denies session
4. Attempt break-glass role assumption without CISO approval ITSM record
column7: SAML assertion without MFA factor results in authentication failure at IdP; IAM Identity Center session denied; no AWS credentials issued; break-glass assumption without ITSM approval is blocked
column14: Zero shared credentials requirement; requires on-premises IdP team participation in test execution

column4: Validate that CloudTrail log files are protected with SHA-256 digest integrity validation and S3 Object Lock prevents deletion or modification
column5: CloudTrail org trail configured with digest files; S3 Object Lock COMPLIANCE mode active on Log Archive bucket
column6:
1. Verify SHA-256 digest files are generated hourly in Log Archive S3 bucket
2. Attempt to delete a CloudTrail log file from Log Archive bucket (expect denial)
3. Attempt to modify (overwrite) a log file in Log Archive bucket (expect denial)
4. Run cloudtrail validate-logs for a 24-hour window
column7: Hourly digest files present in Log Archive S3; delete attempt returns AccessDenied (Object Lock COMPLIANCE mode); overwrite attempt returns AccessDenied; validate-logs returns no integrity failures for all validated log files
column14: ISO 27001 A.12.4 tamper-evident audit trail requirement; critical for regulatory evidence package

column4: Validate that SIEM and ITSM API credentials in Secrets Manager are accessible only by designated Lambda execution roles and rotate correctly
column5: Secrets Manager secrets deployed; SIEM and ITSM credentials populated; Lambda execution roles assigned
column6:
1. Attempt GetSecretValue on SIEM API key secret using a non-designated IAM role (expect denial)
2. Verify designated SIEM forwarding Lambda execution role can retrieve secret
3. Trigger manual rotation on the SIEM API key secret
4. Verify Lambda uses updated credentials post-rotation; confirm SIEM delivery continues
column7: Non-designated role returns AccessDenied on GetSecretValue; Lambda execution role retrieves secret successfully; secret rotation completes without Lambda error; post-rotation SIEM delivery test passes within 5-minute SLA
column14: 90-day rotation policy; validates least-privilege access to secrets per DD Secrets Management design

column4: Validate that all platform Lambda functions have no CRITICAL or HIGH CVE findings outstanding at go-live
column5: AWS Inspector enabled in all relevant accounts; Lambda functions deployed
column6:
1. Enable AWS Inspector for Lambda standard in Management and Audit accounts
2. Run Inspector scan across all cntso-* Lambda functions
3. Triage all findings by severity
4. Remediate any CRITICAL or HIGH findings
5. Re-scan to confirm zero CRITICAL/HIGH at go-live
column7: Zero CRITICAL or HIGH Inspector findings on any platform Lambda function at go-live; all MEDIUM findings documented with remediation timeline; scan report included in compliance evidence package
column14: SOW Phase 3 quality gate requirement: zero CRITICAL/HIGH findings at go-live

column4: Validate that platform services meet the 99.9% availability target over a representative measurement period
column5: CloudWatch monitoring deployed; all platform services operational
column6:
1. Configure CloudWatch availability composite alarm for platform services (Security Hub, Config, EventBridge, Lambda, AFT pipeline)
2. Monitor for 72-hour continuous period during Phase 3
3. Calculate availability percentage from uptime metrics
4. Review any partial-availability periods
column7: Platform services availability ≥ 99.9% over 72-hour measurement period; CloudWatch metrics confirm no unplanned outage windows; any planned maintenance windows excluded from calculation
column14: SOW target: 99.9% platform availability; 72-hour Phase 3 measurement provides initial baseline

column3: DR Failover — RTO and RPO Validation
column4: Validate that the DR failover procedure achieves RTO < 4 hours and RPO < 1 hour as required by the SOW
column5: DR baseline in ap-southeast-4 deployed; S3 CRR active; DR runbook available; CISO and Priya Nair available for drill sign-off
column6:
1. Record current S3 CRR replication lag (confirm < 60 minutes)
2. Simulate ap-southeast-2 failure by switching Security Hub, Config, and CloudTrail aggregation to ap-southeast-4
3. Record drill start timestamp
4. Execute DR failover runbook step-by-step
5. Confirm ap-southeast-4 governance platform operational
6. Record time from drill start to full DR operational state (RTO)
7. Confirm Log Archive data currency in DR region (RPO)
column7: RTO < 4 hours (measured from drill start to DR fully operational); RPO < 1 hour (Log Archive data in ap-southeast-4 is current within 1 hour of drill start); DR test results documented for regulatory evidence package; no data loss in Log Archive
column14: SOW hard requirement; Phase 3 DR drill is regulatory evidence; results included in Deliverable #20

column4: Validate that S3 Cross-Region Replication lag from ap-southeast-2 to ap-southeast-4 meets RPO target under normal conditions
column5: S3 CRR configured on Log Archive bucket; CloudWatch S3 CRR metrics enabled
column6:
1. Enable CloudWatch ReplicationLatency metric for Log Archive bucket CRR rule
2. Monitor replication lag for 24 consecutive hours
3. Record maximum, average, and p95 replication latency values
4. Confirm maximum lag < 3600 seconds (1 hour) throughout 24-hour window
column7: Maximum S3 CRR replication lag < 1 hour (3600 seconds) over 24-hour measurement; average lag < 5 minutes under normal load; CRR alarm threshold at 3600 seconds correctly calibrated; measurement results included in DR evidence
column14: RPO sub-metric; measured independently of DR drill to provide continuous evidence of RPO capability

column4: Validate SIEM forwarding pipeline stability over a sustained 4-hour period with regular finding generation
column5: SIEM forwarding Lambda deployed; synthetic finding generator script available
column6:
1. Configure synthetic finding generator to produce 1 CRITICAL finding every 5 minutes for 4 hours
2. Monitor Lambda error rates, DLQ depth, and X-Ray traces throughout
3. Monitor SIEM receiving end for all 48 expected findings
4. Record Lambda cold start frequency and p95 duration
column7: All 48 synthetic findings delivered to SIEM within 5-minute SLA; Lambda error rate = 0%; DLQ depth remains 0; Lambda p95 duration remains stable (no memory leak indicators); all findings received by SIEM with no gaps
column14: Extended stability test; validates no Lambda memory or concurrency issues over sustained period

column4: Validate that all ~80 Config rules in the ISO 27001 conformance pack are in ACTIVE evaluation status and that no rules have evaluation errors
column5: Config conformance pack cntso-iso27001-org deployed in Audit account
column6:
1. List all Config rules in the ISO 27001 conformance pack
2. Verify all rules are in ACTIVE state
3. Identify any rules with evaluation errors
4. Cross-reference rule list against ISO 27001 Control Mapping Matrix (Deliverable #4)
5. Confirm coverage of all required Annex A control domains
column7: All ~80 Config rules in ACTIVE evaluation status with zero evaluation errors; conformance pack maps to all ISO 27001 Annex A control domains documented in Deliverable #4; coverage report included in compliance evidence package
column14: ISO 27001 compliance framework requirement; directly supports regulatory evidence package

column4: Validate that CloudTrail and Config logs meet the 12-month WORM retention requirement
column5: Log Archive S3 bucket with Object Lock COMPLIANCE mode active; logs from Phase 2 onward present in bucket
column6:
1. Inspect S3 Object Lock configuration on Log Archive bucket (confirm COMPLIANCE mode, 365-day retention)
2. Verify KMS SSE-KMS encryption is applied to all log objects
3. Check S3 bucket policy denies non-HTTPS requests (aws:SecureTransport condition)
4. Attempt to delete a log object with root credentials (expect denial)
5. Run cloudtrail validate-logs for a representative 7-day window
column7: S3 Object Lock in COMPLIANCE mode with 365-day retention period; all objects SSE-KMS encrypted; HTTPS-only bucket policy confirmed; root credential delete attempt returns AccessDenied; validate-logs returns zero integrity failures
column14: ISO 27001 A.12.4 and A.18 requirements; validates tamper-evident audit trail for regulatory submission

column3: Compliance Posture Review in Security Hub
column4: Platform engineer and CISO validate the Security Hub compliance dashboard and confirm it provides a usable compliance posture view for the regulatory review

2. Navigates to Security Hub in Audit account
3. Reviews summary compliance score for ISO 27001 standard
4. Drills into a specific non-compliant finding; reviews ISO 27001 control mapping
5. Exports a compliance report in PDF format

column14: SOW go-live criterion: ISO 27001 evidence package validated by CISO; CISO must confirm report is audit-submission-ready

column4: Platform engineer validates the ability to query and export CloudTrail audit logs for a specific user and time window
column5: CloudTrail org trail delivering to Log Archive S3 bucket; platform engineer has SecurityViewer permission set
column6:
1. Platform engineer logs in via SSO portal using SecurityViewer permission set
2. Navigates to CloudTrail in Log Archive account

4. Verifies that session tags (PrincipalTag/email) are present in event records

column3: SCP Guardrail Confirmation by Platform Team
column4: Platform team validates that SCPs are active and preventing prohibited actions in production accounts before go-live sign-off
column5: SCPs deployed on production OUs; platform engineer has test account in production OU with account-admin role
column6:
1. Platform engineer attempts to create an IAM user in production account (expect AccessDenied)
2. Platform engineer attempts to launch resource in us-east-1 from production account (expect AccessDenied)
3. Platform engineer confirms these denials to CISO as evidence that guardrails are effective
4. CISO reviews the corresponding CloudTrail AccessDenied events in Log Archive
column7: Platform engineer independently confirms SCP effectiveness; CISO reviews CloudTrail evidence of SCP denials; platform team accepts guardrail posture as meeting internal security baseline; result documented in UAT sign-off record
column14: SOW go-live criterion: 100% production accounts governed by SCPs; must be confirmed by platform team not Amatra

column4: Platform engineer validates that the Security Hub compliance dashboard and finding workflow are intuitive for day-to-day operations
column5: Security Hub dashboard populated with representative findings; training session TRN-005 completed by platform team
column6:
1. Platform engineer navigates to Security Hub without guidance
2. Identifies the top 3 highest-severity CRITICAL findings
3. Marks a finding as suppressed with a business justification
4. Generates an insight chart showing compliance trend over the past 7 days
5. Provides usability feedback to Amatra facilitator
column7: Platform engineer completes all navigation steps without assistance; finding triage workflow is understood; suppression with justification is successfully recorded; trend chart generated; platform engineer rates usability ≥ 4.0 / 5.0
column14: UX acceptance criteria; gather formal feedback for post-go-live guardrail tuning

column3: SIEM Alert Validation by Security Team
column4: SIEM team validates that Security Hub CRITICAL and HIGH findings are correctly received and correlated in the on-premises SIEM
column5: SIEM integration deployed and tested; SIEM team has query access to their platform
column6:
1. Amatra generates a synthetic CRITICAL Security Hub finding in the Audit account
2. SIEM team queries their SIEM platform for the finding within 5 minutes
3. SIEM team confirms finding payload is correctly formatted for their correlation rules
4. SIEM team confirms CloudTrail high-risk events (DeleteTrail test event) are also received

column7: SIEM team independently confirms findings received within 5-minute SLA; finding payload format is correct for existing SIEM correlation rules; CloudTrail events received and identifiable; SIEM team provides written integration acceptance for go-live sign-off
column14: SOW go-live criterion: Security Hub findings forwarded to SIEM validated by SIEM team; written acceptance required

column4: Platform team dry-runs the Account Vending operational runbook end-to-end to confirm runbook accuracy and self-sufficiency
column5: Operational Runbook Suite (Deliverable #21) delivered and accepted; sandbox environment available
column6:
1. Platform engineer follows Account Vending runbook step-by-step in sandbox (no Amatra guidance)
2. Platform engineer raises ITSM request, approves it, monitors AFT pipeline, validates new account baseline
3. Platform engineer records any steps that are unclear or require clarification
4. Amatra PM and platform lead review runbook feedback and update if required
column7: Platform engineer completes runbook without assistance on second dry-run attempt; all runbook steps are accurate and executable; any first-attempt issues documented and resolved; Priya Nair accepts runbook as operationally ready
column14: SOW go-live criterion: operational runbooks delivered and accepted by Priya Nair; dry-run validation is acceptance gate

column4: CISO and platform engineer dry-run the Break-Glass Access operational runbook to confirm the emergency procedure is understood and executable
column5: Break-glass runbook delivered; sandbox Management account configured; CISO and platform engineer available
column6:
1. CISO and platform engineer read the break-glass runbook
2. Platform engineer simulates raising ITSM emergency access request in sandbox
3. CISO simulates approval of emergency access request
4. Platform engineer confirms GuardDuty finding and SIEM alert would be triggered (reviewed via test output)
5. Platform engineer confirms post-event credential rotation steps
6. CISO accepts runbook as compliant with internal security policy
column7: CISO and platform engineer can execute break-glass procedure independently from the runbook; CISO accepts runbook as meeting internal security policy requirements; any gaps identified are corrected before go-live; CISO provides written runbook acceptance
column14: CISO acceptance required for M8 sign-off; break-glass procedure is a key ISO 27001 A.8 control evidence item

column4: CISO and Priya Nair formally validate all SOW go-live readiness criteria are met and provide written go-live approval (M8 milestone)
column5: All functional and non-functional tests completed; all UAT scenarios passed; ISO 27001 evidence package ready; operational runbooks accepted
column6:
1. Amatra QA Lead presents go-live readiness checklist to CISO and Priya Nair
2. CISO and Priya Nair review test results for each go-live criterion
3. CISO confirms: SCPs active; zero shared credentials; Security Hub SIEM validated; DR drill passed; evidence package accepted
4. Priya Nair confirms: runbooks accepted; team self-sufficient; account vending operational
5. Both provide written sign-off on go-live approval
column7: All go-live readiness criteria met (zero outstanding CRITICAL defects; SCPs active; IAM Identity Center live; SIEM validated; DR drill passed; evidence package accepted; runbooks accepted); written sign-off obtained from both CISO and Priya Nair; M8 milestone achieved
column14: SOW M8 milestone gate; blocks production go-live (M9); both CISO and Priya Nair must sign off in writing

column4: Priya Nair validates that at least one platform engineer can independently perform core operational tasks after completing all knowledge transfer sessions
column5: All training sessions (TRN-001 through TRN-009) completed; sandbox environment available
column6:
1. Platform engineer performs end-to-end account vending without assistance (observed by Priya Nair)
2. Platform engineer navigates Security Hub and exports a compliance report without assistance
3. Platform engineer executes DR failover runbook in sandbox and documents simulated RTO
4. Platform engineer diagnoses a simulated AFT pipeline failure using CloudWatch logs and runbook
5. Priya Nair rates platform engineer competency
column7: Platform engineer completes all four tasks without Amatra assistance; Priya Nair confirms platform team self-sufficiency; any competency gaps addressed with targeted follow-up sessions before M10 hypercare end; assessment results documented in Deliverable #22
column14: SOW knowledge transfer success metric: platform team self-sufficient by Week 24; observation by Priya Nair is acceptance criteria
